name: autocannon (Node.js HTTP Benchmarking)

agent_readiness.notes: Node.js HTTP/HTTPS benchmarking tool. autocannon -c 10 -d 30 http://localhost:3000. -c connections, -d duration, -p pipelining. JSON output: autocannon --json. Programmatic API: const result = await autocannon({url, connections, duration}). POST with body: --body '{"key":"value"}'. Custom headers: -H 'Authorization: Bearer TOKEN'. Pairs with Clinic.js for profiling. Created by Matteo Collina (Node.js core team). The standard Node.js HTTP benchmarker.

gotchas: Node.js only (wrk or bombardier for multi-language teams)
HTTP/1.1 pipelining differs from HTTP/2 multiplexing
Benchmarking same machine adds noise (benchmarkee and benchmarker compete for resources)
Doesn't simulate complex user scenarios (use k6 for that)
best_when: Clinic.js integration for performance profiling under load
Quick API latency and RPS measurement
Programmatic benchmarking in CI/CD with JSON output
avoid_when: When wrk or bombardier provides better cross-language consistency
summary: autocannon is the standard Node.js HTTP benchmarking tool from Matteo Collina (Node.js core team). autocannon -c 100 -d 30 http://localhost:3000. JSON output for CI. Programmatic API. HTTP pipelining support. Pairs with Clinic.js for profiling under load. Quick Latency/RPS/throughput stats. Use vegeta for constant-rate testing, k6 for complex scenarios.
